item.title: Japan's cherry blossom picnics feel the pinch of global inflation
item.description: Global inflation is taking a bite out of Japan's iconic "hanami" cherry blossom picnics, with an index tracking food and вҖӢdrink costs up 25% since 2020, private think tank Dai-ichi вҖҢLife Research Institute said on Tuesday.

item.title: Japan's SMFG plans for possible takeover of Jefferies, FT reports
item.description: Japan's Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group is working on вҖӢplans for a possible takeover of U.S. investment bank Jefferies , the вҖҢFinancial Times reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Foreign outflows hit Asian stocks as Iran war drives oil shock fears
item.description: Asian stocks have seen heavy foreign outflows so far in March as disruptions to Middle East energy supply from the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran stoked fears of an oil shock вҖӢand stagflation risks.

item.title: SK Hynix may raise up to $10 billion from US listing, Korea Economic Daily says
item.description: South Korea's SK Hynix is considering raising 10 trillion to 15 trillion won ($10.03 billion) through a potential U.S. listing, aiming вҖӢto expand production capacity for advanced memory chips, the вҖҢKorea Economic Daily reported.

item.title: Feed cost surge from Iran war deepens pain for China's pig farmers
item.description: Rising grain prices fuelled by the Iran war вҖӢare lifting animal feed costs in China, the world's biggest pig market, piling pressure on producers already hit вҖҢby weak demand and hog prices at 16-year lows.

item.title: Fevertree Drinks' profit drops 16% on Molson Coors' partnership impact, UK levy dispute
item.description: Fevertree Drinks posted a 16% drop in annual вҖӢprofit on Tuesday, reflecting the impact вҖҢof a disputed 2.8-million-pound (3.76 million) packaging levy and margin pressure from the early вҖӢstages of its U.S. partnership вҖӢwith Molson Coors .

item.title: Ford to recall over 254,600 U.S. vehicles over issues with assistance features, NHTSA says
item.description: Ford Motor вҖӢis recalling вҖҢ254,640 vehicles in вҖӢthe U.S. вҖӢover issues that вҒ may вҖӢresult in вҖӢthe loss of rearview camera вҖӢimage вҖӢand some advanced вҖҢdriver вҒ assistance features, the U.S. National вҖӢHighway вҖӢTraffic вҒ Safety Administration вҖӢsaid вҖӢon вҒ Tuesday.

item.title: Samsung Electronics, union to resume talks over bonus, union says
item.description: Samsung Electronics' management вҖӢand its South вҖҢKorean workers' union have agreed to вҖӢresume talks вҖӢon bonuses, the union вҒ said in вҖӢa statement on вҖӢTuesday.

item.title: Oracle reworks its finance, procurement apps for AI agents
item.description: Oracle is revamping its cloud-based financial software used by large companies to work with artificial intelligence agents, with a goal of having humans вҖӢask the system business questions and letting AI figure out how to вҖҢfind the data.

item.title: Broadcom flags supply constraints, says TSMC capacity a bottleneck
item.description: Chip designer Broadcom said it is seeing supply chain constraints, including capacity limits at its manufacturing partner TSMC , highlighting the ripple вҖӢeffects of soaring demand for AI chips on the broader tech вҖҢindustry.

item.title: Morning Bid: Little relief from Trump
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: European car sales rise modestly in February, Tesla reverses year-long skid
item.description: New car sales in Europe rose in February after falling in January, while sustained demand for electric vehicles helped Elon вҖӢMusk's all-electric brand Tesla resume growth for the first time since вҖҢDecember 2024, official data showed on Tuesday.

item.title: In Iran fallout, US shares hold up better than global rivals, for now
item.description: In the market fallout from the Iran war, U.S. equities have held up better than their counterparts in other global regions, but that relative strength may not shield them from вҖӢmore severe declines should the Middle East conflict drag on.

item.title: China positions itself as a stable economic force amid global uncertainty at Beijing forum
item.description: Global corporate executives attending China's flagship annual business conference this week were вҖӢreassured by leaders of the world's second-largest economy that it remains a predictable anchor at a time of geopolitical flux and global вҖҢuncertainty.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon says AWS' Bahrain region 'disrupted' following drone activity
item.description: Amazon said on Monday its Amazon Web вҖӢServices region in Bahrain has been "disrupted" amid вҖҢthe current conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: SK Hynix: to buy EUV scanners for $8 billion from ASML Korea
item.description: SK вҖӢHynix will вҖҢacquire an extreme вҖӢultraviolet вҖӢscanner from ASML вҒ Korea вҖӢfor 11.95 вҖӢtrillion won ($7.97 billion) to вҖӢprepare вҖӢfor mass production вҖҢof вҒ new products, the company вҖӢsaid вҖӢin вҒ a regulatory вҖӢfiling on вҖӢTuesday.

item.title: Alibaba develops next-gen chip for agentic AI, Chinese media says
item.description: Alibaba on Tuesday revealed its next-generation XuanTie C950 5-nanometer processor at an internal conference on Tuesday, local вҖӢmedia reported, as the Chinese tech giant gears up вҖҢfor the shift towards agentic AI.

item.title: Highlights of EU-Australia trade agreement
item.description: The EU and Australia finalised a long-awaited free trade deal on Tuesday as Europe seeks to diversify its export markets and expand ties beyond its traditional partners.

item.title: Citi appoints two new co-chiefs for infrastructure financing division
item.description: Citi said on Tuesday it has appointed Eric Farina and вҖӢRob Cascarino as co-heads of the вҖҢInfrastructure Financing & Capital Solutions Group (IFCS).

item.title: Exclusive: ZYT readies AI that can outdriveВ its own CEO on Shenzhen streets
item.description: The chief executive of Chinese autonomous drive startup ZYT says the AI system his company is about to debut is already a better driver than вҖӢhe is on the crowded streets of Shenzhen.

item.title: Dollar nurses losses as markets weigh Trump delay in Iran strikes
item.description: The dollar nursed steep losses against major вҖӢcurrencies on Tuesday in a wild start to the week after U.S. President Donald Trumpdelayed the вҖҢbombing of Iran's power grid, a move that allayed fear of a prolonged war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil rises as markets assess supply risks after Iran denies US talks
item.description: Oil prices rose in early trade on Tuesday on вҖӢsupply fears, as Iran denied it had held talks with the United States to end the war in вҖҢthe Gulf, contradicting President Donald Trump, who said a deal could be reached soon.

item.title: Shares rally, oil retreats as Trump extends Iran ultimatum
item.description: Asian stocks rallied, oil prices nursed losses and the dollar wobbled on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump postponed the bombing вҖӢof Iran's power grid, allaying fear of a deeper energy shock.

item.title: Japan's core inflation slows below BOJ target, complicates rate communication
item.description: Japan's core consumer inflation slowed below the central bank's вҖӢ2% target in February for the first time in nearly four years, data showed, as government fuel subsidies offset rising import costs вҖҢfrom a weak yen and surging oil prices from the Iran war.

item.title: Gilead to buy biotech firm Ouro Medicines in over $2 billion deal
item.description: Gilead вҖӢSciences said вҖҢon вҖӢMonday вҖӢit would вҒ buy вҖӢprivately вҖӢheld biotech firm вҖӢOuro вҖӢMedicines in вҖҢa вҒ deal worth вҖӢup вҖӢto $2.18 вҒ billion.

item.title: US crude oil futures rise over $1 in early Asia trade
item.description: U.S. crude futures rose вҖӢmore than $1 in вҖҢearly Asia trade on Tuesday as вҖӢmarkets assessed вҖӢthe supply outlook after вҒ Iran denied вҖӢholding talks with вҖӢthe United States, contradicting President Donald Trump's вҖӢearlier claim вҖӢthat discussions were under вҖҢway.

item.title: Jury in social media addiction trial tells judge it's having difficulty coming to consensus
item.description: The jury in the social media addiction trial in Los Angeles told the judge вҖӢon Monday it is having difficulty вҖӢcoming to a consensus with one вҒ defendant.

item.title: Apollo's private credit fund limits investor withdrawals after redemption requests surge
item.description: Apollo Global's business development company, Apollo Debt Solutions, said on Monday that it is limiting redemptions after вҖӢinvestors sought to withdraw approximately 11.2% of shares outstanding.

item.title: FCC banning imports of new Chinese-made routers, citing security concerns
item.description: The Federal Communications вҖӢCommission said on вҖҢMonday it was banning the import вҖӢof all вҖӢnew foreign-made consumer routers, вҒ the latest вҖӢcrackdown on Chinese-made вҖӢelectronic gear.

item.title: Fed's Daly: no single most-likely path for rates
item.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary вҖӢDaly on Monday said вҖҢthat unless the Iran conflict resolves quickly and the вҖӢFed can simply "look through" вҖӢa temporary increase in oil вҒ prices, it is вҖӢnot clear what the Fed's вҖӢnext move on interest rates will need to be.
